title: Ensure That 'Firewalls & Networks' Is Limited to Use Selected  Networks Instead of All Networks

input_row.title: Ensure That 'Firewalls & Networks' Is Limited to Use Selected  Networks Instead of All Networks
input_row.description: Limiting your Cosmos DB to only communicate on whitelisted networks lowers its attack footprint.
input_row.rationale: Selecting certain networks for your Cosmos DB to communicate restricts the number of networks including the internet that can interact with what is stored within the database.
input_row.audit: From Azure Portal 1. Open the portal menu. 2. Select the Azure Cosmos DB blade 3. Select a Cosmos DB to audit. 4. Select Networking. 5. Under Public network access, ensure Selected networks is selected. 6. Under Virtual networks, ensure appropriate virtual networks are configured. From Azure CLI Retrieve a list of all CosmosDB database names: az cosmosdb list For each database listed, run the following command: az cosmosdb show For each database, ensure that isVirtualNetworkFilterEnabled is set to true From Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: 862e97cf-49fc-4a5c-9de4-40d4e2e7c8eb - Name: 'Azure Cosmos DB accounts should have firewall rules'
input_row.remediation: From Azure Portal 1. Open the portal menu. 2. Select the Azure Cosmos DB blade. 3. Select a Cosmos DB account to audit. 4. Select Networking. 5. Under Public network access, select Selected networks. 6. Under Virtual networks, select + Add existing virtual network or + Add a new virtual network. 7. For existing networks, select subscription, virtual network, subnet and click Add. For new networks, provide a name, update the default values if required, and click Create. 8. Click Save.
input_row.impact: WARNING: Failure to whitelist the correct networks will result in a connection loss. WARNING: Changes to Cosmos DB firewalls may take up to 15 minutes to apply. Ensure that sufficient time is planned for remediation or changes to avoid disruption.
input_row.default_value: By default, Cosmos DBs are set to have access all networks.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control focuses on ensuring that "Firewalls & Networks" is limited to selected networks rather than allowing access from all networks. This concept applies primarily to network access control settings, which are directly supported by AWS services such as Amazon RDS (Relational Database Service) or Amazon DynamoDB.
- AWS provides API calls to query access control configurations for database services.
- The rationale emphasizes minimizing the attack surface by restricting network access. This is consistent with AWS best practices for resource security.
- The audit process can effectively leverage AWS APIs via the boto3 SDK or the AWS CLI to check for the desired configurations.
- Therefore, this control can be fully automated through API checks.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_rds_db_instance_network_access_restrictions_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the AWS CLI command `aws rds describe-db-instances` or `boto3` method `rds.describe_db_instances()` to retrieve a list of all RDS DB instances.
- For each DB instance, check the `DBInstanceAttribute["DBInstanceStatus"]` and `VpcSecurityGroups` attributes to determine if network access restrictions are implemented.
  - Specifically, ensure that the `VpcSecurityGroups` associated with the RDS instances do not allow access from all networks (e.g., that the `IpRanges` in the associated security group should not include `0.0.0.0/0`).
- The expected configuration should include security groups that have tight ingress rules specifying allowed source IP ranges.
- Example Pass/Fail criteria:
  - Pass: VPC security groups associated with RDS have no entries allowing `0.0.0.0/0` in their ingress rules.
  - Fail: Presence of `0.0.0.0/0` as an allowed ingress entry in VPC security groups associated with any RDS instances.
